title: corythophora rimosa subsp. rimosa
intro: Corythophora rimosa subsp. rimosa, also known as 'Dragon's Blood', is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation due to its tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Corythophora rimosa subsp. rimosa include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and prevent infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to respiratory issues.
